mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, immediate consumer spending contractions and recession fears trigger short-term equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE > 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive quarterly equity returns through improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive monthly equity returns during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The predictive power of INDPRO reverses sign or becomes statistically insignificant (t-stat < 1.5) during late-cycle or contraction regimes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening labor market conditions, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS changes remains below 0.02, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220.0, markets price in imminent monetary tightening and margin compression, triggering immediate short-term equity declines over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are non-negative in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL > 220.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of positive payroll growth and declining unemployment rates confirms a robust labor market expansion, reducing recession probabilities and supporting positive quarterly equity returns through sustained earnings growth.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE changes yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample forecasting models over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound environment, accommodative monetary policy combined with positive GDP growth signals sustains equity valuations via liquidity effects and risk-on sentiment, producing positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: The predictive relationship between FEDFUNDS and forward returns vanishes or reverses when FEDFUNDS rises above 1.0%, indicating the mechanism is strictly bound to the ZLB regime.
